mechanism_narrative: Monthly growth in real retail sales (RSAFS) directly captures household consumption strength, which drives corporate revenues and predicts positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 20-day forward returns following positive RSAFS momentum are not significantly greater than zero (t-test p > 0.05) over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: An unemployment rate below 6.0% signals labor market tightness that increases aggregate wage income and consumption expenditure, producing positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 produces negative 60-day returns in more than 50% of months when UNRATE is below 6.0%.

mechanism_narrative: Weak industrial production paired with strong payroll employment indicates a services-led economy where labor income sustains aggregate demand despite manufacturing softness, generating positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Joint z-score combination of INDPRO and PAYEMS fails to deliver positive Rank IC over any 3-year window, or the interaction term is not statistically significant (p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: During late-cycle expansions, accelerating consumer price inflation signals imminent monetary tightening, raising discount rates and equity risk premiums, which produces negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: In identified late-cycle periods, positive CPIAUCSL surprises do not produce negative 20-day returns in at least 55% of instances.

mechanism_narrative: A federal funds rate above 0.5% reflects active monetary tightening that increases borrowing costs and reduces equity risk premiums, leading to negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 forward 20-day returns are non-negative in more than half of the months when FEDFUNDS exceeds 0.5%.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating industrial production reflects improving manufacturing output and capacity utilization, which boosts corporate operating leverage and drives positive S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Positive INDPRO momentum does not correspond to positive 5-day returns with Rank IC > 0.03 over a 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous strength in housing starts and retail sales creates a reinforcing demand loop where residential investment drives downstream consumption; the joint movement of these series predicts positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The interaction term of HOUST and RSAFS has no incremental explanatory power (F-test p > 0.05) for 20-day returns beyond individual series.

mechanism_narrative: In a post-crisis recovery regime, sustained payroll employment growth absorbs labor slack and lifts household income, generating positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: In post-crisis recovery periods, positive PAYEMS surprises fail to produce positive 60-day returns in more than 55% of instances.
